summary: First iteration: validated OpenFrame MVP (FastAPI + React). Backend: 7/7 pytest tests pass covering all 6 endpoints including 3 real Claude LLM calls (parse-cobol, map-schema, validate-data), the pipeline simulator with Mongo persistence, and the /api/runs read (no _id leakage). Frontend: all 5 pages render, LLM-driven flows (Parser, Mapper, Validation) return UI-ready results, Pipeline simulator streams 4 stages (EXTRACT/CONVERT/TRANSFORM/LOAD) with summary stats, tabs switch (rules/input/anomalies), no console errors.

integration_issues.issue: Minor UX: on dashboard mount the empty-state is briefly visible before /api/runs resolves (runs default is []). After a pipeline run, navigating back to / can show the empty state for ~1-2s until the GET /api/runs request completes. Not a bug (runs do appear once resolved and backend /api/runs returns them correctly), but a skeleton/loading state would be more polished.

action_items: (Optional polish) Add a loading skeleton to Dashboard Recent Runs so empty state doesn't flash before /api/runs resolves.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: _extract_json falls back to searching for first {...}/[...] — good resilience; consider also stripping leading/trailing non-JSON chatter even without fences (currently handled by the fallback).

server.py: /api/pipeline/run uses random.seed(hash(source_file + target)) — Python hash() is randomized per-process (PYTHONHASHSEED), so results are only deterministic within a single backend process. Consider hashlib for stable seeding if reproducibility across restarts matters.
server.py: CORS origins read from env with default '*' — fine for preview; tighten for production.

server.py: validate-data passes sample_rows[:50] to LLM — good safeguard. parse-cobol has no input size cap though; a very large copybook could drive token cost up. Consider a max-length guard (e.g. 50k chars).
Server file is 432 lines including models — within limits, but if more endpoints are added consider splitting models into backend/models.py and routes into routers/.
test_credentials: None required — EMERGENT_LLM_KEY present in /app/backend/.env; no user auth in app.
seed_data_creation: Pipeline simulator created real persisted runs in Mongo (TEST_CUSTOMERS.VSAM → postgres.TEST_run_persist). Mapper persisted 'test_customers' event. These are safe to leave or clean up by main agent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 APIs pass in /app/backend/tests/test_openframe_api.py (run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var). LLM calls take ~7-15s each — keep timeouts generous. The map-schema LLM normalizes table_name to snake_case (documented in test). Frontend data-testids verified: dashboard-page, hero-launch-btn, pipeline-card-01..04, stat-*, runs-empty, parser-page, parser-input, parser-run-btn, parser-reset-btn, parser-copy-btn, parser-empty, parser-loading, field-row-N, target-postgres/bigquery/snowflake, mapper-run-btn, pipeline-run-btn, cfg-ebcdic, cfg-packed, cfg-normalize, validation-run-btn, tab-rules, tab-input, tab-anomalies, quality-score.
